frameworks.name: categorical prior / Bayesian feature inference
frameworks.one_line: categorical judgment supplies a prior used in downstream sensory recall and inference

frameworks.name: direct working-memory modification
frameworks.one_line: alternative that categorical judgments rewrite sensory representations in working memory

frameworks.name: selective memory recall / sample rejection
frameworks.one_line: only memory samples consistent with the categorical judgment are accepted for inference

core_question: Do categorical judgments directly modify sensory representations in working memory, or do they bias estimates via downstream inference?
key_assumptions: subjects can revert an incorrect categorical judgment given feedback
feature estimation combines memory samples with a categorical prior
manipulations: forced reversal of the initial categorical judgment after feedback
comparison of estimates between trials with and without judgment reversal
key_findings: no trace of the initial (incorrect) categorical judgment remained in feature estimates after reversal
subjects flexibly switched to the corrected categorical prior to produce estimates
cross-validated model comparison favored selective recall of judgment-consistent memory samples
interpretations: categorical judgments do not modify working-memory sensory content
biases arise downstream as top-down expectations shaping recall and inference
